flips.rationale_pt: C.3: Abstract explicitly states 'agentic AI framework' with automated decision-making for infrastructure monitoring. Misclassified as llm_as_workload when core contribution is agentic AI system (Boundary A). Should be Include per RQ1.
flips.evidence_quote: This work presents an agentic AI framework that integrates Federated Learning (FL) for Large Language Models (LLMs) and open-vocabulary object detection (OVD) to automate roadside infrastructure monitoring.

flips.rationale_pt: C.3: LLM explicitly enables 'autonomous decision-making' for task priority adjustment, dynamic resource allocation, and anomaly handling in UAV command system. Core contribution is LLM-based RM decisions (Boundary A), not serving LLM workload (Sub-modo A) nor analyzing logs (Sub-modo B).
flips.evidence_quote: LLM integration enhanced decision-making accuracy across multiple dimensions—task priority adjustment (76.3% to 94.7%), dynamic resource allocation (68.9% to 91.2%), and anomaly handling (71.5% to 93.8%); Integrating large language models into unmanned aerial vehicle command information systems enables autonomous decision-making

audit_methodology.c1_search: Scanned 192 classical_agents Exclude papers for explicit 'agentic AI' mentions. Found 0 papers (papers mentioning 'intelligent agent' or 'autonomous agent' alone do not qualify as Boundary A per protocol Sub-agente 1 §Critérios de decisão).
audit_methodology.c2_search: Scanned 68 rl Exclude papers for LLM-aided/guided/assisted/enhanced patterns combined with RL. Found 0 papers.
audit_methodology.c3_search: Audited 27 llm_as_workload Exclude papers by reading full abstracts to distinguish 'LLM serves (Sub-modo A)' vs 'LLM uses RM (override)' vs 'LLM decides RM (C.3 flip)'. Applied Sub-agente 2 override logic: papers where LLM clearly decides RM autonomously should be Include. Found 2 papers with high-confidence evidence.
audit_methodology.confidence_threshold: Only 'high' confidence flips reported. Medium/low borderline cases kept as Exclude per cautious interpretation of llm_as_workload category.
